lessonInfo.title: Lesson 3 - The Power of Small Acts

words.ex: I pulled down my jacket from the hanger.
I get off at the next stop.
We are ready for the trip.
He left his umbrella on the bus.
Can you wake up before 7 a.m.?
The stars are bright in the night sky.
The children are excited for Christmas.
He cut in line at the store.
She took off her sunglasses.
I stayed home because of the rain.
We screamed on the roller coaster.
We laughed a lot.
We enjoyed the ride.
The ride was exciting.
Let's ride the roller coaster!
We stood in line for 30 minutes.
He called his friends.
He stood in front of us.
He said sorry and went to the back.
The man held the door for us.
He held the door open.
The man was very kind.
Small acts can make people happy.
It's the magic of manners.
Good manners are important.
We went to the magic show.
The magician was on the stage.
The magician did amazing tricks.
May I pull down the blind?
Don't leave your trash.
We went to an amusement park.
He smiled at me.
He went back to the end of the line.
Small acts make people happy.
Many people were waiting.
